figures.name: Athanasius
figures.title: Defender of Orthodoxy
figures.quote: Athanasius contra mundum (Athanasius against the world).
figures.summary: The 20th Pope of Alexandria who fiercely defended the Nicene Creed against Arianism, suffering five exiles for his uncompromising stance.

figures.name: Antony the Great
figures.title: Father of Monks
figures.quote: Wherever you go, have God always before your eyes.
figures.summary: An Egyptian Christian who retreated to the desert, pioneering the eremitic (solitary) monastic lifestyle that inspired a massive movement.

figures.name: Pachomius
figures.title: Founder of Cenobitic Monasticism
figures.quote: Be careful not to be deceived by the enemy.
figures.summary: Established the first structured, communal (cenobitic) monasteries in Upper Egypt, creating a rule that shaped Western monasticism.

figures.name: Apollos
figures.title: Alexandrian Apologist
figures.quote: He powerfully refuted the Jews in public, showing by the Scriptures that the Christ was Jesus. — Acts 18:28
figures.summary: An eloquent, highly educated Jewish Christian from Alexandria who became a major evangelist and defender of the faith in the early church.
